name: Double-Loop Learning
description: Argyris & Schön: single-loop modifies parameters within a fixed framework; double-loop modifies the framework itself. A control system tuning its gains is single-loop; realizing that the entire controller architecture is wrong is double-loop. The transition is governed by the unexplainability threshold: when accumulated anomalies exceed the framework's predictive capacity, the governing assumptions must be questioned.

name: Association & Causation Theory
description: Association (correlation) is undirected and symmetric: A and B co-occur. Causation is directed and asymmetric: A produces B. The gap is bridged by four constraints: temporal ordering, mechanism chain, intervention validation, and boundary condition satisfaction. Without all four, the edge remains associative with a decaying confidence. Tending toward chaos theory: in nonlinear systems, local causality can break down under sensitive dependence on initial conditions.

name: Dimensionality Reduction & Information Constraint
description: Any representation of a system with fewer degrees of freedom than the original is a form of compression. This is not merely neural network distillation; it is the fundamental operation of science itself: from PDE to ODE, from many-body to mean-field, from high-dimensional data to low-dimensional manifold. The constraint is the loss function: what information is deemed essential and what is discarded. This is an epistemic choice, not just a technical one.

name: Convergence & Stability Theory
description: Not specific to control systems. Any iterative process—gradient descent, belief propagation, phase transition, ecological succession—must satisfy: (1) a Lyapunov-like function that decreases monotonically, (2) a basin of attraction that prevents divergence to infinity, (3) a rate of convergence that is finite and preferably bounded. The mathematics is the same whether the system is a neural network, a crystal lattice, or a predator-prey ecosystem.

name: Hierarchy & Emergence
description: Complex systems organize into layers where each layer's behavior is not reducible to the layer below. Thermodynamics emerges from statistical mechanics; fluid dynamics from molecular collisions; control performance from hardware physics. The key is the separation of timescales: fast processes (vibration, thermal diffusion) are treated as constraints on slow processes (structural deformation, tool wear). This is the foundation of multiscale modeling and hierarchical control.

name: Epistemic Boundary & Unexplainability
description: The threshold beyond which a system's behavior cannot be predicted by its current model. This is not ignorance; it is a formal boundary. In control: the unmodeled dynamics. In materials: the defect-dominated regime. In chemistry: the reaction pathway bifurcation. When unexplainability exceeds the threshold, the model must be expanded or replaced. The chaos suppression parameter: if a proposed framework change affects >30% of the knowledge graph, auto-revert to conservative single-loop.
